_doc: User verdicts for the 86-row derm_contamination_candidates.csv. Order matches CSV row order. Each verdict is one of KEEP_IN_DERM | MOVE_TO_TRIAGE | RELABEL_ONLY | DROP. new_label (if set) replaces the gold level on apply (for both MOVE and RELABEL_ONLY).

verdicts.notes: Real derm case — circular red itchy thigh lesion, prior tinea-pattern. Nephrology flag is a false positive (regex artifact).
RLQ abdominal pain x10 years with extensive negative workup including virtual colonoscopy. No derm content. Pure triage/GI; gold GREEN is fine.

Post-ejaculation hematuria + right varicocele/hydrocele in 36yo with single kidney. Urology question. UPGRADE YELLOW: persistent hematuria with negative cystoscopy + solitary kidney warrants ongoing nephrology surveillance.
Allergic rhinitis x10y, on nasal spray. ENT/allergy. No derm content. GREEN stands.
Drug-induced acne is unambiguously dermatology. Pulmonology flag is false positive (likely regex on 'drug').
Splenic cyst + bloating. GI/general surgery, no derm content. GREEN defensible (asymptomatic stable cyst).
8.4mm renal cyst incidental finding. Nephrology not derm. GREEN stands (small simple renal cysts routinely benign).
Old TB fibrotic shadow on CXR, employment medical clearance. Pulmonology not derm. GREEN stands.
Hair loss x2yr in known hypothyroid patient. Hair loss IS dermatology; endocrine flag is contextual mention, not chief complaint.
CXR with faint infiltrates + old TB scars. Pure pulmonology, no derm content. UPGRADE YELLOW: faint new infiltrates in someone with TB history needs pulmonology follow-up to rule out reactivation.
ALT 95 (>2x ULN) + high cholesterol + cancer anxiety. Hepatology, no derm content. UPGRADE YELLOW: persistent ALT elevation needs hepatology workup.
Cervical pigmented spots, doctor explicitly arranged a derm skin-check. Patient asking about skin-cancer/cervical-cancer connection. Legitimate derm consult.
Androgenic alopecia in 23yo with PCOS. Hair loss IS dermatology with endocrine context.
Post-TB lung scars, pulmonology not derm. GREEN stands.
Swelling at TB test site (PPD induration question). Chief complaint IS a skin reaction, even if related to TB.
Birth-control overdose. Pure toxicology / poisoning query, no derm content. UPGRADE YELLOW: overdose is same-day Poison Control / GP.
Acne + oily skin + premenstrual abd pain + infertility. Acne is the chief complaint with hormonal context.
Calcific density in right lung on CXR. Pure pulmonology. GREEN defensible (likely old granuloma).
Abdominal pain + mild itching, Hodgkin's worry. GI workup negative. Itching mentioned but chief complaint is abdominal pain + lymphoma anxiety. GREEN defensible.
Nighttime cough + neck/jaw itching. Itch on neck/jaw is cutaneous; cough is associated. Possible eczema-asthma overlap.
Post-myomectomy unplanned pregnancy with uterine rupture concern. Pure OB/GYN, no derm content. YELLOW stands.
Post-herpes-zoster persistent abdominal pain (likely post-herpetic neuralgia in dermatomal distribution) + insomnia in elderly. Herpes is dermatology; abd pain is the dermatomal sequela.
Chronic cough x60 days + dyspnea. Pure pulmonology, no derm content. YELLOW stands.
Post-renal-transplant patient asking about HSV transmission, with painful genital sores + abdominal vesicular lesions. Real derm presentation (HSV reactivation in immunocompromised).
Body-wide red spots + fever + body aches + abd pain + diarrhea (likely viral exanthem). Skin findings central.
Cough + skin nodules + nail-bed swelling + weight loss + fatigue x150d. Multi-system but skin and nail findings are prominent. Possible sarcoidosis/connective tissue disease.
Antibiotic-induced rash + body itching + elevated LFTs. Drug eruption is dermatology.
On IV Rocephin x2wk for bacteremia + post-discharge UTI, asking about pink discharge. No derm content; ortho flag is regex artifact. YELLOW stands.
Pediatric fever + sore throat + abd pain + petechiae on legs. Petechiae IS skin finding. HSP/meningococcal differential.
Pediatric hepatomegaly + anorexia + vomiting on USG. Pure hepatology/GI. YELLOW stands.
Pulmonary nodule on CT + intermittent fever x4yr. Pure pulm/onc. YELLOW stands (pulmonary nodule needs surveillance).
AST 56 + ALT 91 + weight gain + psoriasis on methotrexate. Psoriasis IS the active dermatologic problem; LFTs are methotrexate monitoring.

Recently discharged after blood clot in right thigh, now post-travel hot/painful lump in right buttock. Suspected recurrent DVT/cellulitis post-discharge. Borderline derm vs vascular but skin warmth/swelling IS a derm presentation. UPGRADE RED: keep in derm with RED label as a hard test case.
Mediastinal lymphadenopathy on CT awaiting biopsy + anxiety. Pure pulm/onc, no derm content. YELLOW stands.
Fever 101.9 + lethargy + confusion + memory loss + dizziness + visual changes in post-hysterectomy patient. No derm content. YELLOW stands (defensible YELLOW given subacute pattern).
Post-exercise dyspnea + dizziness + tingling + pallor in deconditioned exerciser. Pure cardio/pulm. YELLOW stands.
Confirmed peripheral neuropathy on EMG with progressive symptoms. Pure neurology. YELLOW stands.
Pediatric rectal bleeding + GRAY stool. Acholic (pale) stool in a child is the key finding — biliary obstruction/atresia differential. Pure hepatology/peds-GI. YELLOW stands.
Chronic respiratory + dizziness + chest/abd pain x365d. Multi-system but no derm content. YELLOW stands.
Poison ivy progressing to purpura + pus + lymph node swelling x7d. Worsening contact dermatitis with secondary infection. Toxicology flag is false positive on 'poison.'
Shingles + persistent itch + fever + abd pain. Shingles IS dermatology.
Abdominal pain + fever + skin sensitivity. Possible early herpes zoster prodrome with abd dermatomal pain.
Right shoulder injury + swelling + immobility + severe pain x2d. Pure ortho. YELLOW stands.
Diabetic elderly with leg burning, itching, and swelling. Prior wound history. Possible diabetic dermopathy/cellulitis.
Chronic post-chemical-inhalation (Harpic+bleach) respiratory symptoms x30d. Pure pulm/toxicology. YELLOW stands.
Perineal lump + yellow penile lesion + urethral itching. Genital skin lesions are dermatology/STI workup.
Hep C + abd pain + suboxone + psych meds. Pure hepatology/addiction medicine. YELLOW stands.
Mother bitten by dog at 12wk gestation; baby now 2wk old with congenital heart defects. Causation question, no current derm. YELLOW stands.
Multi-system: fatigue + alopecia + dry skin + elevated LFTs + vaginal hyperplasia. Hair loss + dry skin are dermatologic chief complaints in a multi-system presentation.
Pneumonia x21d on third antibiotic course + chest discomfort + fatigue. Pure pulm. YELLOW stands.
Wasp sting causing finger swelling + redness in T2DM. Sting reaction IS dermatology.
Tongue ulcers + swelling + difficulty speaking/eating in 21yo on migraine meds. Aphthous ulcers / drug-induced oral lesions are dermatology / oral medicine.
Pediatric fever + rash + platelet variability (initially low at 131, now 644). Rash is dermatologic chief complaint with hematologic findings — Kawasaki / measles / viral exanthem differential.
Evening low-grade fever x180d + hypertension + diabetes (insulin) + fistula treatment. Multi-system, no derm content. Possible chronic infection or endocarditis differential. YELLOW stands.
Recurrent UTI + painful vulvar/clitoral blisters x120d. Genital blisters are dermatologic chief complaint (possible HSV).

Isolated SGPT 97 (mildly elevated), no symptoms. Pure hepatology, no derm content. DOWNGRADE GREEN: isolated mild ALT elevation in asymptomatic patient is routine repeat-and-monitor. Gold over-triage.
Elderly with 1wk fever + old TB scars on CXR (no active TB). Pure pulm/general medicine. YELLOW stands.
Phimosis with foreskin tearing/bleeding in 19yo. Genital skin pathology.
ESLD with intractable pruritus + insomnia + depression. Pruritus IS the dermatologic chief complaint, even though etiology is hepatic.
Low body temp 97.4 + nausea + abd pain + headache + weakness x7d in known gallstone patient. Pure GI/general medicine. Cholangitis/sepsis differential. YELLOW stands.
Severe abd cramps + dark bleeding + tissue passage post-DMPA injection. Pure GYN. YELLOW stands.
Sudden 2-min syncope with apnea + pallor + HTN 160/100. Cardiac/neurology emergency. RED stands.
Elderly with rapid post-discharge decompensation: incontinence + confusion + inability to walk/eat after pulmonary edema admission. ICU-level. RED stands.

Patient on ventilator with breast skin infection progressing to BLACK necrotic blisters + pulmonary edema. Black blisters are dermatologic — necrotizing fasciitis differential. RED preserved. Hard test case where derm presentation drives the emergency.
Sternal mass + severe abd pain + nausea + heartburn + bedbound in pre-cancer patient. Pure GI/onc emergency. RED stands.

Hair loss with active suicidal ideation and prior suicide attempt in 19yo. The crisis is psychiatric, not dermatologic. RED stands. NOTE: hair loss alone would be derm GREEN/YELLOW; the suicidality is what makes it RED. Worth flagging this case as an example for the safety classifier — model should escalate to mental-health crisis path.
Veet hair-removal cream causing genital irritation + hematuria. Chemical contact derm with mucosal involvement is the chief complaint. RED preserved (hematuria after topical chemical = same-day GP).
Diabetic admitted with fever + chest pain + pneumonia + elevated LFTs. Already in hospital. Pure pulm/cardiology emergency. RED stands.
Severe HTN 200/115 + palpitations + tinnitus + Trousseau sign + Hashimoto history. Pheochromocytoma/carcinoid screen indicated. Pure cardio/endo emergency. RED stands.
45yo with altered mental status + GTC seizure + parietal hypodensity on CT. Pure neurology emergency. RED stands.
Recurrent TB + fungal infection + pneumothorax + chest drain in elderly. Already in hospital, pulm critical care. RED stands.

Toddler post-pneumonia with yellow-tinted feet + strange smell + iron deficiency. Skin discoloration of feet IS dermatologic finding. Could reasonably be GREEN (likely carotenemia or iron-deficiency-related).
10yo with body-wide hives + warm sensation + asthma history on inhaler/Singulair. Hives IS dermatology. YELLOW stands.
Pediatric low-grade fever + loose stool + abd pain + nasal congestion + oral herpes lesion x4d. Oral herpes IS dermatologic. YELLOW stands.
Sudden marble-sized hand swelling that resolved leaving a bruise. Transient localized swelling + ecchymosis is dermatologic (urticaria, insect bite, vasculitis, or transient soft-tissue swelling).

Persistent cough + fever + leg imbalance after recurrent TB + penicillin exposure. Pulm/neurology presentation, no derm content. RED stands (TB recurrence + neurologic symptoms = TB meningitis differential).
Carcinoid syndrome with BP swings 80/38 → 158/126, heart valve damage, pulmonary atelectasis, lymphadenopathy. Pure cardio/pulm/onc emergency. RED stands.
Pruritic red spotted rash spreading from legs to arms + abnormal CBC (mild leukocytosis, anemia). Rash IS dermatology with hematology context.

Faint white line from breast to umbilicus in 7yo adopted child. The 'line' is likely linea alba (normal anatomic finding) or a fading scar — not a discrete dermatologic lesion. Endocrine flag false positive. Borderline keep-vs-move; lean MOVE. GREEN stands.
LLQ pain + constipation + cancer recurrence concern in elderly. Pure GI/onc. YELLOW stands.
Post-stroke elderly with new bedsores + uncontrolled diabetes. Bedsores ARE dermatology / wound care. YELLOW stands.

Bilateral renal swelling + dysuria + nausea + breast pain + itching in 22yo. Multi-system but the 'itching' appears to be in the renal/flank area, not a dermatologic chief complaint. Pure nephrology presentation. YELLOW stands.
Hard moving abdominal mass + severe abd pain + painful defecation, 'hand and feet moving inside' (likely peristaltic mass or hernia). Pure GI/surgery. YELLOW stands.
Body itching + abd pain + bloating + glans pain after travel to Costa Rica with tap water exposure. Body itching IS dermatologic (parasitic differential).
Post-trauma perineal swelling + red lump + severe pain. Pure surgical (likely perianal abscess). YELLOW stands (perianal abscess needs incision and drainage).

Multi-system: dysphagia + denture problems + extremity rashes/tingling + memory loss + word-finding difficulty + extremity swelling. Has skin signal but the chief complaint is neurologic emergency (possible Guillain-Barré, stroke, or systemic vasculitis with neuro involvement). RED stands.
